chapter: Chapter 10: The Human Eye and the Colourful World

questions.question: What is the function of the human eye?
questions.answer: It enables us to see objects by detecting light.

questions.question: What is the retina?
questions.answer: The light-sensitive layer at the back of the eye where images are formed.

questions.question: What is the function of the pupil?
questions.answer: It regulates the amount of light entering the eye.

questions.question: What is the role of the iris?
questions.answer: It controls the size of the pupil to adjust light entering the eye.

questions.question: What is accommodation of the eye?
questions.answer: The ability of the eye lens to change its focal length to see objects clearly at different distances.

questions.question: What is the near point of the human eye?
questions.answer: The nearest distance at which objects can be seen clearly, usually about 25 cm.

questions.question: What is the far point of a normal human eye?
questions.answer: Infinity.

questions.question: What is myopia?
questions.answer: A defect of vision in which distant objects appear blurred.

questions.question: How is myopia corrected?
questions.answer: By using a concave lens.

questions.question: What is hypermetropia?
questions.answer: A defect of vision in which nearby objects appear blurred.

questions.question: How is hypermetropia corrected?
questions.answer: By using a convex lens.

questions.question: What is presbyopia?
questions.answer: Loss of accommodation of the eye due to ageing.

questions.question: What are bifocal lenses?
questions.answer: Lenses with two different focal lengths used to correct presbyopia.

questions.question: What is dispersion of light?
questions.answer: The splitting of white light into its component colours.

questions.question: What is the spectrum of light?
questions.answer: The band of colours obtained after dispersion of white light.

questions.question: Name the colours of the visible spectrum.
questions.answer: Violet, Indigo, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, Red.

questions.question: What is scattering of light?
questions.answer: The deflection of light in different directions by particles in a medium.

questions.question: Why does the sky appear blue?
questions.answer: Due to scattering of shorter wavelength blue light by atmospheric particles.

questions.question: Why does the Sun appear reddish during sunrise and sunset?
questions.answer: Because shorter wavelengths are scattered and longer red wavelengths reach the eye.

questions.question: What is Tyndall effect?
questions.answer: The scattering of light by particles in a colloidal solution.

questions.question: What is the role of the ciliary muscles?
questions.answer: They adjust the shape of the eye lens for focusing.

questions.question: What happens when light enters the eye?
questions.answer: It is refracted by the eye lens and forms an image on the retina.

questions.question: Which part of the eye sends visual signals to the brain?
questions.answer: The optic nerve.

questions.question: What type of image is formed on the retina?
questions.answer: Real and inverted image.

questions.question: What is the cause of atmospheric refraction?
questions.answer: Variation in the density of air layers in the atmosphere.
